input.headline: Will there be enough turkeys for Christmas in Ireland this year?

raw.headline: Will there be enough turkeys for Christmas in Ireland this year?

winner: Why did the Irishman bring a ladder to the supermarket on Thanksgiving? He heard there was a shortage of turkeys and wanted to reach the goose section!

candidates.text: In Dublin, they're worried there won't be enough turkeys for Christmas this year. Well, as a local said, "It's not the end of the world... unless you're a turkey.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the context of Ireland. It uses a pun on 'end of the world' with a turkey twist, which is both clever and humorous."}

candidates.text: Why did the Irish leprechaun bring a turkey to his potluck? He heard it was the bird of the year.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses a leprechaun for an Irish twist, but the connection to turkeys and Christmas in Ireland is weak. The 'bird of the year' line is predictable and lacks surprise."}

candidates.text: Why did the Irishman bring a ladder to the supermarket on Thanksgiving? He heard there was a shortage of turkeys and wanted to reach the goose section!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks strong specificity and surprise. The reference to Thanksgiving rather than Christmas in Ireland feels slightly off-topic, and the humor relies on a mild pun that may not land as strongly."}

candidates.text: Will there be enough Irishmen for a turkey this Christmas?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with wordplay but lacks the punch and surprise of a strong holiday-themed pun."}

candidates.text: Why did the Irishman bring a map to the supermarket? To find the flock-on-the-merry.
